def_zh: 波特的行业结构分析框架:一个行业的长期盈利能力由五种力量决定——现有竞争者的对抗、新进入者威胁、替代品威胁、买方议价力、供方议价力。战略的第一课:选对行业往往比经营得好更重要。
def_en: Porter's industry-structure lens: long-run profitability is set by five forces — rivalry, threat of entry, threat of substitutes, buyer power and supplier power. Strategy's first lesson: which industry you're in often matters more than how well you run.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: force 源自拉丁 fortis（强壮）——“力”即强度；波特1979年《哈佛商业评论》成文，把产业组织经济学翻译成经理人语言。

synonyms.sentence: Industry structure explains why airlines starve and software feasts.
Structural analysis asks not who is winning but why winning is possible at all.

example: Before betting on a market, a partner runs the five forces: strong buyers and easy entry mean margins will bleed no matter how good the operator.
nuance: 五力分析行业不分析公司——它解释行业为何赚钱，不解释某家公司为何领先（后者靠 competitive-advantage）。静态框架，须配合动态变化使用。

def_zh: 五力之一:潜在进入者随时可能瓜分利润，除非存在进入壁垒（规模经济、品牌、专利、牌照、资本门槛）。高利润行业若无壁垒，利润会被新玩家迅速摊薄。
def_en: One of the five forces: newcomers can arrive and compete away profits unless barriers to entry (scale, brand, patents, licences, capital) keep them out. High margins without barriers are an invitation.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: barrier 源自古法语 barre（横木、栅栏）——“壁垒”即挡路的横木；经济学把它抽象成任何抬高进入成本之物。

synonyms.sentence: Barriers to entry are the moat measured from the attacker's side.
A contestable market disciplines incumbents with the mere threat of entry.

example: Cloud infrastructure has brutal entry barriers — the capex alone deters all but three hyperscalers.

def_zh: 五力之一:当买家集中、转换成本低、产品同质、信息充分时，买方能压低价格、索取更多——利润从卖方转移到买方。零售巨头对供应商即典型强买方。
def_en: One of the five forces: concentrated, well-informed buyers facing low switching costs and commoditised products can squeeze price and terms — profit migrates from seller to buyer. Big-box retail over its suppliers is the textbook case.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: bargain 源自古法语 bargaignier（讨价还价、经商）——议价力即“讨价还价的筹码”。

synonyms.sentence: Customer concentration is a discount the buyer has not yet demanded.
Galbraith's countervailing power pits big buyers against big sellers.

example: Supplying a single dominant retailer feels like revenue until it renegotiates: buyer power turns your margin into its promotion.

def_zh: 五力之一:当供应商集中、投入品不可替代、转换成本高时，供方能抬价或限量，压缩买方利润。上游垄断（如光刻机、稀土、关键芯片）是供方权力的极端形态。
def_en: One of the five forces: concentrated suppliers of irreplaceable inputs can raise prices or ration supply, squeezing their customers' margins. Upstream chokepoints — lithography machines, rare earths, leading-edge chips — are supplier power at its sharpest.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: supply 源自拉丁 supplere（填满、补足）——供应即“补足需求的缺口”；供方权力则是控制这份补给。

synonyms.sentence: Input concentration lets one supplier tax an entire industry.
A supply chokepoint converts a technical monopoly into geopolitical leverage.

example: One Dutch firm makes the machines that make advanced chips — supplier power so concentrated it became foreign policy.

def_zh: 五力之一:满足同一需求的不同产品设定了价格天花板——不是直接竞品，而是另一种解法。视频会议是商务航班的替代品，流媒体是影院的替代品。
def_en: One of the five forces: different products meeting the same need cap your price — not rivals but alternative solutions. Video calls substitute for business flights; streaming substitutes for cinemas.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: substitute 源自拉丁 substituere（置于其下、替换）——替代品即“顶上来站位之物”。

synonyms.sentence: Substitution risk is the competitor that isn't in your industry classification.
Substitutes set a price ceiling no marketing budget can lift.

example: Taxis benchmarked each other while the real substitute — ride-hailing plus transit apps — rewrote the price ceiling.

def_zh: 五力的核心:现有企业间的对抗强度。高固定成本、产品同质、增长停滞、退出壁垒高时，价格战与产能过剩会吞噬全行业利润。
def_en: The centre of the five forces: how fiercely incumbents fight. High fixed costs, undifferentiated products, stalled growth and high exit barriers breed price wars and overcapacity that devour industry profit.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: rival 源自拉丁 rivalis（共用同一条河 rivus 的人）——竞争者本义“争水者”；对岸取水，冲突自来。

synonyms.sentence: A price war is rivalry that has run out of other ideas.
Overcapacity turns a good industry into a charity for customers.

example: Airlines combine high fixed costs, perishable inventory and no differentiation — a structural recipe for rivalry that torches returns.
nuance: rival 词源“争同一条河”——竞争的隐喻从来是稀缺资源之争；与教材 involution（内卷）同构:过度对抗，无人得利。

def_zh: 波特把企业拆成一串增值活动:进料物流、生产、发货、营销、服务（主要活动）+ 采购、技术、人力、基础设施（支持活动）。竞争优势来自某些环节比对手做得更便宜或更好。
def_en: Porter decomposes a firm into value-adding activities: inbound logistics, operations, outbound logistics, marketing, service (primary) plus procurement, technology, HR and infrastructure (support). Advantage lives in doing some links cheaper or better than rivals.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: chain 源自拉丁 catena（锁链）——价值链即环环相扣的增值活动；每一环都可能是优势或漏点。

synonyms.sentence: An activity system wins when the links reinforce each other, not just individually.
The value system extends the chain upstream and downstream, supplier to buyer.

collocations: where value is created and captured
example: Ask of every activity: does this create value the customer pays for, or just cost? The value chain forces that audit link by link.

def_zh: 波特论断:可持续优势只有三条路——总成本领先、差异化、专注（聚焦细分市场）。同时追求成本与差异化易“卡在中间”（stuck in the middle），两头不到岸。
def_en: Porter's claim: sustainable advantage comes only three ways — overall cost leadership, differentiation, or focus (a niche). Chasing cost and differentiation at once risks being stuck in the middle, winning at neither.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: generic 源自拉丁 genus（类、种）——“基本/通用”战略即适用于所有行业的“类型”；strategy 见 long-march 词源（希腊 stratēgos 将道）。

synonyms.sentence: Strategic positioning is deciding what you will deliberately not do.
Stuck in the middle is the graveyard of firms that wanted to be everything.

collocations: avoid being stuck in the middle
example: A brand that discounts like a value player while advertising like a premium one is stuck in the middle — paying for both, owning neither.

def_zh: 基本战略之一:成为行业最低成本生产者，靠规模经济、经验曲线、流程效率与议价，以同等价格获更高利润或以低价扩张。沃尔玛、廉航、Costco 是典范。
def_en: A generic strategy: be the lowest-cost producer via scale, the experience curve, process efficiency and procurement, then earn more at the same price or grow by charging less. Walmart, low-cost airlines and Costco are exemplars.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: cost 源自拉丁 constare（合计为、值）——成本即“合起来值多少”；领先则是把这个数字压到全行业最低。

synonyms.sentence: A low-cost operator can start the price war it knows it will win.
Scale economics let the biggest player set a floor rivals can't reach.

example: Costco's cost leadership is structural: membership fees, thin SKUs and bulk buying let it undercut everyone and still profit.

def_zh: 基本战略之一:提供买家愿为之付溢价的独特价值——品牌、设计、质量、服务、生态。目标是逃离价格竞争，把对抗从“更便宜”转到“更好/更独特”。
def_en: A generic strategy: offer unique value buyers pay a premium for — brand, design, quality, service, ecosystem. The aim is to escape price competition, shifting the fight from cheaper to better or singular.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: differentiate 源自拉丁 differre（分开携带、区别）——差异化即“使自己被区别对待”，从而定价不同。

synonyms.sentence: Premium positioning sells the difference, then defends it from imitation.
Real differentiation shows up as pricing power on the income statement.

example: Apple's differentiation is total-system: hardware, software and services lock in a premium that pure specs can't explain.

def_zh: 基本战略之一:集中服务某一细分市场（地域、客群或产品线），在窄范围内做到成本领先或差异化——以深度换广度，避开全市场巨头的正面碾压。
def_en: A generic strategy: serve one narrow segment (a geography, buyer group or product line) and win there on cost or differentiation — trading breadth for depth to avoid meeting the giants head-on.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: focus 拉丁语原义“炉膛、焦点”——聚焦即把火力集中于一点；战略上即把资源压向一个细分。

synonyms.sentence: A niche player owns a pond too small for whales to bother draining.
The segment specialist knows one customer better than any generalist can.

example: A B2B firm that serves only regional hospitals can out-focus a global generalist on exactly the features those buyers value.

def_zh: 企业持续获得超额回报的能力——要么以更低成本交付同等价值，要么以同等成本交付更高价值。“可持续”竞争优势能抵御模仿，这才是战略的终极目标。
def_en: A firm's ability to earn returns above its rivals over time — delivering equal value at lower cost, or higher value at equal cost. A sustainable competitive advantage resists imitation; that durability is strategy's ultimate prize.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: advantage 源自古法语 avant（在前）——优势即“身位在前”；竞争优势即持续领先对手的结构性位置。

synonyms.sentence: An edge that everyone can copy is a feature, not an advantage.
Investors fund the unfair advantage, not the good idea.

example: The test of competitive advantage is boring but decisive: can a well-funded rival copy it next year? If yes, it was never advantage.

def_zh: 巴菲特术语:保护企业超额利润免受竞争侵蚀的结构性屏障——无形资产（品牌、专利）、转换成本、网络效应、成本优势、规模。护城河越宽越持久，超额回报越安全。
def_en: Buffett's term for the structural barrier that protects a firm's excess profits from competition — intangibles (brand, patents), switching costs, network effects, cost edge and scale. The wider and deeper the moat, the safer the returns.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: moat 源自古法语 mote（土丘、护城河堤）——城堡的护城河转喻为企业的竞争屏障，巴菲特使其成为投资通用语。

synonyms.sentence: A durable advantage is a moat that time widens rather than silts up.
Investors price defensibility; anyone can price a good quarter.

example: A network effect is the best moat: each new user makes the product better and the next competitor's climb steeper.

def_zh: 阻止新竞争者进入的结构性障碍:规模经济、资本门槛、专利与牌照、品牌忠诚、网络效应、独占分销。壁垒既保护在位者利润，也是护城河的供给侧来源。
def_en: Structural obstacles that keep new competitors out: scale economies, capital requirements, patents and licences, brand loyalty, network effects, locked-up distribution. Barriers protect incumbent profits — the supply side of the moat.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: 見 threat-of-entry；barrier 来自“横木”，经济学扩展为一切抬高进入成本之物。

synonyms.sentence: An entry barrier is a cost the incumbent already paid and the newcomer hasn't.
Regulatory capture turns the rulebook itself into a barrier to entry.

example: Banking licences, capital rules and legacy systems make barriers to entry so high that fintechs mostly rent the incumbents' rails.

def_zh: 产量越大，单位成本越低——固定成本被摊薄、议价力增强、专业化分工深化。规模经济是成本领先与进入壁垒的根本引擎，但过度扩张会遇到规模不经济。
def_en: As output rises, unit cost falls — fixed costs spread, bargaining power grows, specialisation deepens. Scale economies are the engine of cost leadership and entry barriers, though past a point diseconomies of scale set in.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: economy 源自希腊 oikonomia（家政、oikos 家+nomos 治理）——“经济”本义治家；规模经济即“把家做大反而更省”。

synonyms.sentence: The unit cost curve slopes down until complexity bends it back up.
Below minimum efficient scale, you are subsidising your larger rivals.

example: A chip fab costs the same to run at 60% or 95% utilisation — which is why scale economies force the industry toward a few giants.

def_zh: 用同一套资源生产多种产品比分别生产更便宜——共享品牌、渠道、研发、平台。范围经济解释了多元化与平台扩张的成本逻辑，区别于靠“更多同一产品”的规模经济。
def_en: Producing several products from shared resources costs less than making each separately — shared brand, channels, R&D, platform. Scope economies explain diversification and platform expansion, as distinct from scale (more of the same).

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: scope 源自希腊 skopos（目标、观察者，skopein 看）——范围即“视野所及”；范围经济即一套资源覆盖更宽的产品视野。

synonyms.sentence: Shared resources turn a second product line into almost-free upside.
Cross-selling is scope economics realised at the point of sale.

example: Amazon's logistics and cloud spine give it economies of scope: each new business rides infrastructure the others already paid for.

def_zh: 用户越多，产品对每个用户越有价值——电话、社交、市场平台、支付。网络效应制造赢家通吃与自增强的护城河:领先者的领先会自我放大（正反馈）。
def_en: The more users, the more valuable the product is to each — phones, social networks, marketplaces, payments. Network effects create winner-take-all dynamics and a self-reinforcing moat: the leader's lead compounds.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: network = net（网）+ work（活计）——本指渔网的编结，19世纪转指铁路电报之“网”，再抽象为用户互联的价值网。

synonyms.sentence: Metcalfe's law prices a network by the square of its users, roughly.
Demand-side scale is a moat customers dig for you, one signup at a time.

example: A marketplace with liquidity wins twice: more sellers pull more buyers, who pull more sellers — network effects the challenger can't bootstrap.
nuance: 网络效应≠病毒传播:前者是“更多用户使产品更好用”（价值），后者是“用户带来用户”（增长）。二者常同现但机制不同。

def_zh: 客户更换供应商需付出的代价——数据迁移、重新学习、合同违约、集成改造。高转换成本把一次性交易变成经常性收入，是最可靠的护城河之一。
def_en: What a customer must pay to change providers — data migration, retraining, contract penalties, re-integration. High switching costs turn one-off sales into recurring revenue and form one of the most reliable moats.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: switch 源自低地德语 swutsche（细枝、抽打）→ 转向、切换；转换成本即“切换到别家的代价”。

synonyms.sentence: Lock-in is loyalty that survives the disappearance of affection.
Stickiness is what retention looks like from the product's side.

collocations: the cost of ripping and replacing
example: Enterprise software embeds itself in a company's workflows until switching means retraining thousands — the invoice renews itself.

def_zh: 率先进入市场者可锁定稀缺资源、树立标准、建立网络效应与品牌认知、沿经验曲线抢先下滑。但先发亦有先烈风险——教育市场的成本常由快速跟随者收割。
def_en: Being first can lock up scarce resources, set standards, seed network effects and brand recognition, and ride the experience curve early. But first movers also bleed: fast followers often harvest the market the pioneer educated.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: pioneer 源自古法语 paonier（步兵、开路工）——先驱本义“逢山开路的工兵”，红利与伤亡并存。

synonyms.sentence: Incumbency advantage is a first-mover lead that learned to defend itself.
The fast follower lets the pioneer pay tuition, then enrols for free.

example: Search had many first movers; the winner was a fast follower whose algorithm and network effects made being second irrelevant.

def_zh: 历史选择约束未来选项——早期的偶然决定被锁定，即便后来出现更优方案也难改（QWERTY 键盘、制式标准）。战略上，先建立的安装基础与生态会自我强化。
def_en: History constrains the future — early, sometimes accidental choices get locked in even when better options later appear (the QWERTY keyboard, technical standards). Strategically, an installed base and ecosystem, once established, reinforce themselves.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: path 古英语 pæþ（小径）——路径依赖即“先走出的小径决定了后来的路网”；经济史学家 Paul David 以 QWERTY 命名此现象。

synonyms.sentence: Standards lock-in is path dependence wearing an engineering badge.
An installed base is inertia you can put on a balance sheet.

example: The QWERTY layout was designed to slow typists on 1870s machines — and path dependence has kept it on every phone since.

def_zh: 哈默尔与普拉哈拉德:企业独有、难以模仿、可通向多个市场的能力集合——不是产品，而是产品背后的技能与知识（如索尼的小型化、佳能的光学）。战略应围绕核心能力而非业务单元组织。
def_en: Hamel and Prahalad's idea: the unique, hard-to-imitate skills that open many markets — not products but the know-how behind them (Sony's miniaturisation, Canon's optics). Organise strategy around competences, not business units.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: competence 源自拉丁 competere（一同追求、相称）——能力即“胜任、够格”；与 compete（竞争）同根:能力与竞争本是一词两面。

synonyms.sentence: A distinctive capability is what your rivals would need years to grow, not buy.
Protect the crown jewels; outsource the rest.

example: Amazon's core competence isn't retail — it's operating complex logistics and computing at scale, which is why AWS was a natural adjacency.

def_zh: 战略理论:竞争优势源于企业内部稀缺、有价值、难模仿、难替代的资源与能力，而非外部行业结构。与波特“由外而内”相反，RBV 主张“由内而外”。
def_en: A strategy theory: advantage comes from a firm's internal resources and capabilities that are valuable, rare, inimitable and non-substitutable — not from industry structure. Where Porter looks outside-in, RBV looks inside-out.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: resource 源自拉丁 resurgere（再起、涌出）——资源即“可再涌出之物”；RBV 把企业看成资源束（bundle of resources）。

synonyms.sentence: Inside-out strategy starts with what you have, not what the market lacks.
Strategic assets are the resources rivals can neither buy nor build quickly.

example: A pharma firm's real asset isn't a drug but its research platform — the resource-based view explains why the pipeline is valued above any product.

def_zh: 巴尼的资源筛选工具:一项资源要构成持续优势，须同时满足价值（Value）、稀缺（Rarity）、难以模仿（Inimitability）、组织能利用（Organisation）。四项俱全才是可持续优势的来源。
def_en: Barney's screen for resources: to yield sustained advantage, a resource must be Valuable, Rare, costly to Imitate, and the firm must be Organised to exploit it. Only when all four hold is the advantage durable.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: VRIO 为 Value/Rarity/Imitability/Organisation 首字母缩略——咨询与商学院最爱的记忆锚，一词概括 RBV 的检验清单。

synonyms.sentence: A resource audit runs each asset through VRIO and keeps only the survivors.
The inimitability test asks: could a rival copy this cheaply? If yes, discard it.

example: A great brand is valuable and rare, but if the firm isn't organised to exploit it — the O in VRIO — the advantage stays on paper.

def_zh: 提斯的概念:在快变环境中，企业感知机会（sensing）、把握机会（seizing）、重构资源（transforming）的元能力。静态资源会过时，重组资源的能力才是长青优势之源。
def_en: Teece's concept: in fast-changing environments, the meta-capability to sense opportunities, seize them, and reconfigure resources. Static resources decay; the ability to recombine them is the source of lasting advantage.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: dynamic 源自希腊 dynamis（力、潜能）——动态能力即“重组潜能的力量”；与 dynasty、dynamite 同根。

synonyms.sentence: Sensing and seizing is strategy as reflex, not as five-year plan.
Organisational agility is dynamic capability you can observe in a crisis.

example: Netflix's dynamic capability — reinventing itself from DVDs to streaming to studio — mattered more than any single asset it held.

def_zh: 企业向上游（供应）或下游（分销/客户）扩展，把原本外购的环节纳入内部。可控制关键投入、攫取更多价值链利润、保护质量，但牺牲灵活性并增加固定成本。
def_en: Extending the firm upstream (supply) or downstream (distribution/customer), bringing bought-in activities in-house. It can secure key inputs, capture more of the value chain and protect quality — at the cost of flexibility and higher fixed costs.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: integrate 源自拉丁 integrare（使完整，integer 完整之数）——一体化即“把分散环节合为整体”。

synonyms.sentence: Backward integration buys your supplier before it can raise your prices.
Forward integration puts you closer to the customer — and to the margin.

example: Tesla vertically integrated batteries and software when suppliers couldn't deliver — owning the chokepoints its rivals must rent.

def_zh: 在价值链同一层级并购竞争者或相邻产品线，以扩大市场份额、消除竞争、获取规模经济与范围经济。与纵向一体化（沿链条上下）方向正交。
def_en: Acquiring competitors or adjacent product lines at the same level of the value chain to grow share, remove rivalry, and gain scale and scope economies. Orthogonal to vertical integration (up and down the chain).

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: horizontal 源自希腊 horizōn（分界的圆，horizein 划界）——横向即“同一水平线上”的扩张。

synonyms.sentence: Consolidation is horizontal integration told from the industry's point of view.
A roll-up buys a fragmented sector one small rival at a time.

example: When a market fragments into many subscale players, a roll-up strategy uses horizontal integration to manufacture scale economics.

def_zh: 企业边界的基本抉择:某活动应内部自制还是市场外购？取决于交易成本、战略重要性、能力、控制需求与灵活性。核心能力自制，非核心外包，是通行原则。
def_en: The fundamental boundary choice: should an activity be done in-house or bought from the market? It turns on transaction costs, strategic importance, capability, control and flexibility. Make the core, buy the rest is the working rule.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: 这是科斯《企业的性质》(1937) 的实践版:企业存在，正因为某些交易在内部完成比走市场更便宜。

synonyms.sentence: Insourcing brings back the activity you should never have let define you.
Every make-vs-buy call redraws the firm's boundary by one activity.

example: A retailer buys shipping but makes its own logistics software — the make-vs-buy line falls exactly where control creates advantage.

def_zh: 科斯与威廉姆森的理论:市场交易本身有成本——搜寻、谈判、签约、监督、违约。当这些成本高于内部组织成本时，活动就被纳入企业。它解释了企业为何存在、边界在哪里。
def_en: Coase and Williamson's theory: market transactions carry costs — search, negotiation, contracting, monitoring, enforcement. When these exceed the cost of organising internally, the activity moves inside the firm. It explains why firms exist and where their boundaries fall.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: transaction 源自拉丁 trans（越过）+ agere（做）——交易即“做成一桩来回”；科斯1937年之问:既然市场有效，为何还要有公司?

synonyms.sentence: Asset specificity is what makes a supplier hard to replace — and worth owning.
The hold-up problem is why a specialised supplier eventually integrates or gets integrated.

example: When a component is so specialised that only one supplier can make it, transaction cost economics predicts you'll end up owning that supplier.

def_zh: 波特:战略即定位——选择一套独特的活动组合，交付独特的价值。定位的本质是取舍（trade-offs）:决定不做什么，与决定做什么同样重要。
def_en: Porter: strategy is positioning — choosing a unique set of activities to deliver unique value. Its essence is trade-offs: deciding what not to do matters as much as what to do.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: position 源自拉丁 ponere（放置）——定位即“把自己放在何处”；战略定位即在价值-成本空间里选一个可防守的点。

synonyms.sentence: Strategic trade-offs are the fence that keeps imitators out of your position.
A value curve draws where you compete and where you deliberately don't.

example: Southwest's position — no meals, no assigned seats, point-to-point — works because the trade-offs make it painful for full-service rivals to copy.
nuance: 定位（positioning）在营销里指“占领消费者心智”，在战略里指“选择活动组合”——同词两义，波特与特劳特的用法须分清。

def_zh: 波特:可持续优势不来自单个活动，而来自活动之间的相互强化与一致性。竞争者可以模仿一个环节，却难以复制整套环环相扣的系统——这才是难以模仿的根源。
def_en: Porter: sustainable advantage comes not from single activities but from how they reinforce and align. A rival can copy one link, but not the whole interlocking system — which is what makes it hard to imitate.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: fit 古英语 fitt（相配、恰当）——战略契合即活动彼此“咬合”，形成整体大于部分之和。

synonyms.sentence: Activity fit is why copying one feature never copies the strategy.
A reinforcing system defends itself: attack one part and the others compensate.

collocations: hard to imitate as a whole
example: IKEA's flat-pack, self-assembly, warehouse-store and low-cost design all reinforce one another — the strategic fit, not any single choice, is the moat.

def_zh: 把行业内所有产能按单位成本从低到高排列，横轴累计产量、纵轴成本——市场价由边际（最后一个被需要的）产能决定。低成本产能赚取“租金”，高成本产能在周期低谷出局。大宗商品分析的核心工具。
def_en: Ranking all industry capacity from lowest to highest unit cost — cumulative volume on the x-axis, cost on the y — the market price is set by the marginal (last needed) producer. Low-cost capacity earns rent; high-cost capacity exits in downturns. A core tool of commodity analysis.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: curve 源自拉丁 curvus（弯曲）——成本曲线即把产能成本画成一条上行的阶梯线，直观呈现谁在赚钱、谁在赔钱。

synonyms.sentence: The marginal producer sets the price and makes almost nothing doing it.
Your cost position on the curve decides whether a downturn is a threat or an opportunity.

example: In iron ore, being first-quartile on the cost curve means you profit at prices that bankrupt the miners above you.

def_zh: BCG 的经验发现:累计产量每翻一番，单位成本可预测地下降约20–30%——源于学习、流程改进、规模与创新。它是激进定价与抢占份额战略的理论依据:先亏后赚，以量降本。
def_en: BCG's empirical finding: each doubling of cumulative output cuts unit cost by a predictable 20–30% — from learning, process improvement, scale and innovation. It justifies aggressive pricing to grab share: lose now, win on volume-driven cost.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: experience 源自拉丁 experiri（试验、经历，与 experiment 同根）——经验曲线即“做得越多，越便宜”的量化规律。

synonyms.sentence: The learning curve is why the tenth factory costs less than the first.
Cumulative volume, not annual output, drives the cost decline.

example: Solar panels fell 90% in a decade almost exactly along the experience curve — each doubling of installed capacity cut costs on schedule.
nuance: 经验曲线看累计产量（历史总量），规模经济看当期产量（当下规模）——两者常混，但一个是时间积累，一个是瞬时规模。

def_zh: 沿价值链各环节的利润总量分布图——利润往往极不均匀地集中在少数环节。战略洞察在于:份额大的环节未必赚钱，要去利润池最深处布局（如手机制造薄利、应用商店与芯片厚利）。
def_en: A map of where profit pools along a value chain — earnings are usually concentrated, unevenly, in a few links. The insight: the biggest-revenue stage isn't always where the money is; position where the pool is deepest (handset assembly is thin; app stores and chips are fat).

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: Gadiesh 与 Gilbert 1998年《哈佛商业评论》提出——把利润想象成沿价值链分布的“水池”，深浅不一。

synonyms.sentence: Value capture asks not who does the most work but who keeps the most margin.
Margin migration is the profit pool quietly moving to a different link.

example: In computing, assembling PCs became a thin pool while chips and operating systems held the deep one — value migrated to the links with moats.

def_zh: 企业向特定客户承诺交付的独特价值组合——解决什么问题、为谁、凭什么比替代方案更好。清晰的价值主张回答:客户为何选你而非对手或不选。
def_en: The unique bundle of value a firm promises a defined customer — what problem it solves, for whom, and why it beats the alternatives. A sharp value proposition answers why this customer chooses you over a rival or over doing nothing.

sources.chapter: Strategy 战略

etymology: proposition 源自拉丁 proponere（摆在面前）——价值主张即“摆在客户面前的价值提案”。

synonyms.sentence: A customer promise you can't keep is a value proposition with a fuse.
Nail the why-buy in one sentence or the customer writes it for you — badly.

example: A good value proposition survives the elevator test: who it's for, the pain it kills, and why the alternatives lose — in one breath.

def_zh: BCG 的业务组合工具:按市场增长率与相对市场份额把业务分成明星（高增长高份额）、现金牛（低增长高份额）、问号（高增长低份额）、瘦狗（双低）。现金牛供血，投向明星与选中的问号，处置瘦狗。
def_en: BCG's portfolio tool: plotting market growth against relative share yields stars (high-high), cash cows (low growth, high share), question marks (high growth, low share) and dogs (low-low). Milk the cows, feed the stars and chosen question marks, divest the dogs.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 1970年 BCG 创始人 Bruce Henderson 提出——用现金流逻辑管理多元业务，是最早的组合管理工具。

synonyms.sentence: A portfolio matrix decides which businesses fund which.
A cash cow is a business whose best days fund someone else's first.

collocations: stars, cash cows, question marks and dogs
example: A conglomerate uses the BCG matrix to route cash-cow profits into fast-growing stars rather than propping up dogs out of sentiment.
nuance: 批评者指其过度简化（份额≠盈利，增长≠机会）——今多作沟通工具而非决策依据，与更细的 GE-McKinsey 九宫格并用。

def_zh: BCG 矩阵的精细化:纵轴“行业吸引力”、横轴“业务竞争力”，各分高中低成九格。比两维两分更贴近现实，用于优先排序投资、维持或退出，是组合战略的标准工具。
def_en: A refinement of the BCG matrix: industry attractiveness on one axis, business competitiveness on the other, each split high/medium/low into nine cells. Richer than a 2×2, it prioritises businesses to invest in, hold or harvest — a staple of portfolio strategy.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 1970年代 GE 委托麦肯锡为其150个业务单元设计的评估工具——咨询史上最著名的“九宫格”。

synonyms.sentence: The attractiveness-competitiveness matrix scores where to place the next dollar.
An investment priority screen keeps sentiment out of the capital allocation.

example: A diversified group scores each unit on the nine-box to decide, unsentimentally, where the next billion of capex should go.

def_zh: 增长路径的四象限:现有产品×现有市场=市场渗透，现有产品×新市场=市场开发，新产品×现有市场=产品开发，新产品×新市场=多元化（风险最高）。风险随离核心越远而递增。
def_en: Four growth paths: existing product × existing market = market penetration; existing × new market = market development; new product × existing market = product development; new × new = diversification (the riskiest). Risk rises the further you move from the core.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: Igor Ansoff 1957年提出——“战略管理之父”把增长选项系统化为一张2×2。

synonyms.sentence: The product-market matrix ranks growth bets by how far they stray from home.
Market penetration is growth's safest gear: sell more of what you have to whom you already reach.

example: Before entering a new country with a new product, the Ansoff matrix flags it as double-new — the highest-risk quadrant, needing the strongest case.

def_zh: 金与莫博涅:与其在已知市场（红海）血拼，不如通过“价值创新”开创无人竞争的新市场空间（蓝海）——同时降成本又提价值，用 ERRC（剔除-减少-增加-创造）重构行业边界。
def_en: Kim and Mauborgne: instead of bloodying rivals in known markets (red oceans), create uncontested new space (blue oceans) through value innovation — cutting cost and raising value at once, using the ERRC grid (eliminate-reduce-raise-create) to redraw industry boundaries.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: blue ocean（蓝海）对 red ocean（红海，血染的竞争海域）——一组鲜明的隐喻使框架风行全球。

synonyms.sentence: Value innovation refuses the cost-value trade-off the whole industry accepts.
Uncontested market space is a monopoly you built rather than bought.

example: Cirque du Soleil ignored circus norms — no animals, adding theatre — creating a blue ocean between circus and theatre that neither owned.
nuance: 批评:多为事后叙事，难以事前复制；且蓝海终会变红。作为发散思维工具优于作为可执行方法论。

def_zh: 在已知、边界清晰的市场中与对手争夺同一份需求——规则既定，靠更好或更便宜取胜，利润随竞争加剧而变薄，海水被竞争“染红”。大多数行业的常态。
def_en: Fighting over the same demand in a known, bounded market — rules fixed, winning by being better or cheaper, margins thinning as rivalry intensifies until the water runs red. The default condition of most industries.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: red（血红）喻竞争白热化的市场——与 blue ocean 成对，隐喻的力量正在这份视觉对比。

synonyms.sentence: Zero-sum competition splits a fixed pie thinner every quarter.
Commoditisation is the tide that turns every blue ocean red.

example: Once every airline copied the low-cost playbook, the blue ocean turned red — and fares fell to the cost curve's edge.

def_zh: 克里斯坦森:新进入者用更简单、更便宜、起初更差的产品，从低端或新市场切入，随技术改进逐步上攻，最终颠覆只顾服务高端客户的在位巨头。“创新者的窘境”即在位者理性地忽视它。
def_en: Christensen's theory: entrants attack from the low end or a new market with simpler, cheaper, initially inferior products, then improve upward until they upend incumbents fixated on high-end customers. The innovator's dilemma is that ignoring it looks rational — until it isn't.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: disrupt 源自拉丁 dis-（分开）+ rumpere（打破）——颠覆即“打破原有秩序”；该词被硅谷滥用后已近陈词，克氏原义远为精确。

synonyms.sentence: The innovator's dilemma punishes the incumbent for listening to its best customers.
Low-end disruption enters where the incumbent is happy to lose margin.

example: Digital cameras were 'good enough' and cheap; disruptive innovation let them climb until they dissolved the film business entirely.
nuance: 克氏原义严格:并非“任何大变革”，而特指从低端/新市场向上攻的特定路径；媒体把 disruption 泛化为“任何新奇”，是常见误用。

def_zh: 沿现有性能维度改进产品，服务现有主流客户、维持既有商业模式——更快的芯片、更好的相机。在位者通常擅长延续式创新，却对颠覆式创新反应迟钝。
def_en: Improving products along existing performance dimensions for current mainstream customers within the existing business model — faster chips, better cameras. Incumbents excel at sustaining innovation while missing the disruptive kind.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: sustain 源自拉丁 sustinere（从下托住、维持）——延续式创新即“托住”现有轨道，让它更好而非改道。

synonyms.sentence: Incremental innovation compounds quietly and rarely makes the headlines.
Performance improvement wins this year's customers and can lose next decade's market.

example: Adding megapixels was sustaining innovation; incumbents raced up that curve while smartphones disrupted the entire premise of a camera.

def_zh: 克里斯坦森的洞察:客户不是买产品，而是“雇佣”产品去完成一项任务。“人们要的不是四分之一英寸的钻头，而是四分之一英寸的孔。”聚焦任务而非属性，能发现真正的竞争对手与创新方向。
def_en: Christensen's insight: customers don't buy products, they 'hire' them to do a job. 'People don't want a quarter-inch drill; they want a quarter-inch hole.' Focusing on the job, not the attributes, reveals the true competitor and the real innovation.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

pron: jobs to be done /ʤɑbz tɪ bi dən/
etymology: job 源自中古英语 gobbe（一块、一堆活）——“任务”本义“一块要干的活”；JTBD 把购买还原为“客户想干成的那块活”。

synonyms.sentence: The underlying need outlives every product that ever served it.
Define the customer job broadly and your competitive set triples.

example: A commuter 'hires' a morning milkshake to make a boring drive less dull — a JTBD reframing that beats any survey about flavour.

def_zh: 技术性能随投入呈 S 形演进:早期缓慢、中期爆发、后期趋于饱和。当一条 S 曲线接近平台期，颠覆常来自跳上一条新的 S 曲线。管理者的难题是在旧曲线仍赚钱时押注新曲线。
def_en: A technology's performance follows an S-shape as investment rises: slow start, explosive middle, saturating end. As one S-curve plateaus, disruption usually comes from jumping to a new curve. The manager's hard problem is betting on the new curve while the old one still pays.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 曲线形似字母 S 而得名——生物学（种群增长）、社会学（创新扩散）与技术管理共用同一形状。

synonyms.sentence: The technology life cycle rewards those who leave the plateau before it collapses.
Curve-jumping is cannibalising your best product before a rival does it for you.

example: Combustion engines are high on their S-curve — improving slowly at great cost — while electric drivetrains are still on the steep middle of theirs.

def_zh: 麦肯锡增长框架:H1 维护并扩张核心业务（当下现金流），H2 培育新兴高增长业务（未来引擎），H3 播种可能颠覆的选项（长远种子）。三者需并行投资，避免只顾眼前而枯竭未来。
def_en: McKinsey's growth frame: Horizon 1 defends and extends the core (today's cash), Horizon 2 builds emerging high-growth businesses (tomorrow's engine), Horizon 3 seeds options that might transform the company (the long bets). All three need funding at once, lest the present starve the future.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: horizon 源自希腊 horizōn（划界之圆）——三层面即三条“时间地平线”，越远越不确定。

synonyms.sentence: A healthy growth pipeline funds all three horizons, not just the loudest one.
Balancing today and tomorrow is the whole job the three horizons make explicit.

example: A carmaker runs Horizon 1 (petrol cars), Horizon 2 (EVs scaling now) and Horizon 3 (autonomy) in parallel — killing any one starves a different decade.

def_zh: 贝恩 Chris Zook:最可靠的增长来自围绕强大核心业务、一步步进入相邻领域（新地域、新客群、新产品、新渠道），复用现有能力。跳得越远，失败率越高——聚焦可重复的邻近步。
def_en: Bain's Chris Zook: the most reliable growth comes from stepping into adjacencies around a strong core — new geographies, customers, products, channels — reusing existing capabilities. The further the leap, the higher the failure rate; favour repeatable adjacent moves.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: adjacent 源自拉丁 adjacere（躺在旁边）——邻近扩张即“向紧挨着核心的地方迈一步”。

synonyms.sentence: A strong core is the launchpad every reliable adjacency fires from.
A repeatable model turns one successful move into a growth machine.

example: A retailer that masters one format grows by repeating it in adjacent cities — lower risk than leaping into an unrelated business.

def_zh: 面对深度不确定性，不去预测单一未来，而是构建几种内部自洽、差异显著的未来情景，检验战略在各情景下的稳健性。壳牌1970年代用它预判石油危机而闻名。
def_en: Under deep uncertainty, rather than forecasting one future, build several internally consistent, sharply different scenarios and stress-test strategy against each. Shell's use of it to anticipate the 1970s oil shocks made the technique famous.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: scenario 源自意大利 scenario（舞台布景说明，scena 舞台）——情景即“可能上演的剧本”，规划即预排多套剧本。

synonyms.sentence: What-if analysis replaces a single fragile forecast with a robust range.
Robustness testing asks not what will happen but what would still work.

example: Instead of forecasting one oil price, Shell modelled several worlds — so when the shock came, it had already rehearsed the response.

def_zh: 由团队分别扮演竞争者、监管者、客户，模拟战略举措的连锁反应——“如果我们降价，对手会如何回击？”预演博弈动态，避免只做静态的单方分析。
def_en: Teams role-play competitors, regulators and customers to simulate how a strategic move ripples out — 'if we cut price, how does each rival retaliate?' It rehearses the game-theoretic dynamics that static, one-sided analysis misses.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 军事术语 war game（兵棋推演）借入商业——把地缘战略的“红蓝对抗”搬进会议室。

synonyms.sentence: Competitive simulation reminds you the board plays back.
Red-teaming assigns smart people to attack your own plan on purpose.

example: Before a price cut, a war-game had one team play the rival — and revealed the retaliation that would have turned a share grab into a margin bloodbath.

def_zh: 平台不自产价值，而是撮合两组或多组用户在其上交易与互动（商家-顾客、司机-乘客、开发者-用户），靠网络效应做大。价值创造从“管道”（自产自销）转向“平台”（协调他人）。
def_en: A platform doesn't produce the value itself; it matches two or more user groups to transact and interact on it (merchants-shoppers, drivers-riders, developers-users), scaling on network effects. Value creation shifts from pipes (make and sell) to platforms (orchestrate others).

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: platform 源自法语 plateforme（平的表面、台）——商业平台即供各方“站上来交易的台”。

synonyms.sentence: The pipe-to-platform shift trades making things for orchestrating those who do.
A marketplace is a platform whose product is other people's supply.

example: The most valuable firms increasingly own no inventory: they run platforms where others' supply meets the platform's demand.
nuance: 平台的启动难题是“先有鸡还是先有蛋”:没有商家就没有顾客，反之亦然——冷启动策略（补贴一侧、单边先行）是关键。

def_zh: 平台连接两类相互依赖的用户群，一侧的规模提升另一侧的价值（跨边网络效应）。定价常不对称:补贴价格敏感的一侧（如读者、乘客），向价值高的一侧收费（如广告主、商家）。
def_en: A platform links two interdependent user groups where each side's size raises the other's value (cross-side network effects). Pricing is often asymmetric: subsidise the price-sensitive side (readers, riders) and charge the side that values access (advertisers, merchants).

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 经济学家 Rochet 与 Tirole（诺奖）奠定其理论——解释了为何信用卡、报纸、操作系统能“一边亏本一边赚钱”。

synonyms.sentence: A cross-side network effect means more of them makes it better for us.
Choosing the subsidy side is the platform's most important pricing decision.

collocations: the money side vs the subsidy side
example: A card network gives cardholders rewards (subsidised side) and charges merchants fees (money side) — classic two-sided pricing.

def_zh: 当网络效应、规模经济与转换成本足够强时，市场趋于集中到一两家主导者，第二名与第一名差距悬殊。这类市场里，抢占份额、快速达到临界规模的竞速逻辑压倒一切。
def_en: When network effects, scale economies and switching costs are strong enough, a market tips toward one or two dominant players, with a huge gap to the runner-up. In such markets, the race to grab share and hit critical mass overrides everything.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 借自选举术语 winner-take-all（胜者全得）——经济学用它描述“赢家拿走绝大部分市场”的技术市场结构。

synonyms.sentence: Winner-take-most is the honest version: the leader takes most, not quite all.
Tipping is the moment a contested market picks its monopolist.

example: Social networks tip winner-take-all: once a platform holds everyone's friends, a marginally better rival still can't pull them across.

def_zh: 企业不再只做产品，而是编排一群互补者（开发者、供应商、伙伴）共同为客户创造价值，并从中占据关键节点（如操作系统、应用商店、支付）。竞争从公司对公司升级为生态对生态。
def_en: A firm orchestrates a web of complementors (developers, suppliers, partners) that jointly create value for customers, while occupying the key nodes (OS, app store, payments). Competition escalates from firm-versus-firm to ecosystem-versus-ecosystem.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: ecosystem 源自生态学（希腊 oikos 家 + systēma 系统）——商业生态即“互相依存的商业物种群落”。

synonyms.sentence: Complementors make your product more valuable without being on your payroll.
The orchestrator sets the rules and takes a cut of everyone else's effort.

example: A phone maker's real product is its ecosystem — apps, accessories, services — each of which makes leaving costlier than the hardware alone.

def_zh: 斯莱沃斯基:利润会从过时的商业模式流向更贴合客户优先级的新模式——价值先从A迁往B，再从B迁往C。识别价值正在流向何处、及时布局，是持续盈利的关键。
def_en: Slywotzky's idea: profit flows away from outdated business designs toward those better aligned with customer priorities — value migrates from A to B, then B to C. Spotting where value is heading, and positioning early, is the key to enduring profit.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: migration 源自拉丁 migrare（迁移）——价值迁移把利润想象成会“迁徙”的候鸟，追随客户需求越冬。

synonyms.sentence: A business design is how a firm captures value, and it expires.
Margin migration is value migration seen on the income statement.

example: In computing, value migrated from hardware to software to services to platforms — each incumbent that clung to the old design was left with revenue but no profit.

def_zh: 奥斯特瓦德的一页九格工具:客户细分、价值主张、渠道、客户关系、收入来源、关键资源、关键活动、关键伙伴、成本结构。用一张图讲清“如何创造、交付、捕获价值”。
def_en: Osterwalder's one-page, nine-block tool: customer segments, value propositions, channels, customer relationships, revenue streams, key resources, key activities, key partners, cost structure. It captures how a business creates, delivers and captures value on a single sheet.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: canvas 源自拉丁 cannabis（大麻布，画布材料）——画布即“可涂改的草图”，鼓励快速迭代商业设计。

synonyms.sentence: A revenue model answers the one question investors always circle back to.
The nine blocks force you to admit which part of the model is still a guess.

example: A startup sketches its business model canvas in an hour and instantly sees the weak block: a value proposition with no proven revenue stream beneath it.

def_zh: 回答“我们应经营哪些业务、如何管理这组业务”——区别于业务层战略（“某业务如何在其市场取胜”）。核心问题:多元化是否创造价值、总部如何为各业务增值、如何配置资本。
def_en: Answers 'which businesses should we be in, and how do we manage the set' — as distinct from business strategy ('how does this unit win in its market'). Its core questions: does diversification create value, how does the centre add value to units, and how is capital allocated.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: corporate 源自拉丁 corpus（身体）——公司层战略即管理“企业整体这具身体”，而非单个器官。

synonyms.sentence: The corporate centre must earn its cost by adding value units couldn't create alone.
Portfolio logic asks whether the whole is worth more than the sold-off parts.

example: Corporate strategy asks the ruthless question: would each business be worth more owned by someone else? If yes, the group is destroying value.

def_zh: 如何配置资本与注意力于多个业务:哪些投资成长、哪些维持挤现金、哪些出售。好的组合平衡现金流与增长、风险与回报，并持续修剪不再契合的业务。
def_en: How to allocate capital and attention across multiple businesses: which to grow, which to hold and milk, which to sell. A good portfolio balances cash flow against growth and risk against return, continually pruning businesses that no longer fit.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: portfolio 源自意大利 portafoglio（携带 portare + 纸张 foglio）——本指“文件夹”，引申为一组持有的资产或业务。

synonyms.sentence: Capital allocation is the CEO's most consequential and least visible job.
Active portfolio management means selling winners into strength and cutting losers early.

example: A conglomerate that treats its businesses as a portfolio sells a strong cash cow at a good price to fund three faster-growing bets.

def_zh: Goold 等人:多元集团的总部（母公司）只有当它能比其他所有者更好地为下属业务增值时，才应拥有该业务。否则业务应被出售——母公司在“摧毁价值”。检验多元化的严苛标尺。
def_en: Goold and colleagues: a diversified group's centre (the parent) should own a business only if it adds more value to it than any other owner could. Otherwise the business should be sold — the parent is destroying value. A stringent test of diversification.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: parent 源自拉丁 parere（生育）——母公司即“生养子业务者”；母合优势即“当好家长”的能力。

synonyms.sentence: The better-owner test asks whether anyone could parent this business better than you.
A value-adding centre earns its overhead; a value-destroying one is a tax on its own units.

example: Private equity often applies the parenting-advantage logic in reverse: buy businesses whose current corporate parent is destroying their value.

def_zh: 进入新的产品-市场领域以分散风险、追逐增长或利用范围经济。相关多元化（复用能力）通常创造价值；非相关多元化（纯财务组合）常遭“多元化折价”，除非母公司确有母合优势。
def_en: Entering new product-market arenas to spread risk, chase growth or exploit scope economies. Related diversification (reusing capabilities) usually creates value; unrelated diversification (a pure financial mix) often suffers a diversification discount unless the parent has a real parenting advantage.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: diversify 源自拉丁 diversus（转向不同）+ facere（做）——多元化即“使自己变得不同、分散”。

synonyms.sentence: Related diversification reuses a capability; unrelated diversification just spends cash.
Peter Lynch called value-destroying expansion 'diworsification'.

example: When a firm diversifies into a business sharing no capability with its core, investors usually apply a discount — they can diversify more cheaply themselves.

def_zh: 多元化集团的市值常低于其各业务分拆后的估值之和——因复杂性、资本错配、透明度低与总部成本。这是拆分、剥离与激进投资者要求“分家”的核心动因。
def_en: A diversified group often trades below the sum of its parts if broken up — owing to complexity, capital misallocation, poor transparency and head-office cost. This gap drives break-ups, spin-offs and activist demands to split the company.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: conglomerate 源自拉丁 conglomerare（滚成一团，glomus 线球）——企业集团即“滚在一起的多元业务团”；折价即市场对这团“看不清”的惩罚。

synonyms.sentence: A sum-of-the-parts valuation reveals the discount the market is charging for complexity.
Break-up value is what an activist waves at a sleepy board.

example: Activists target conglomerates trading at a discount, arguing that a spin-off would let the market value each clean business on its own merits.

def_zh: 战略应建立在少数几项相互强化、世界级的差异化能力之上，且所有业务都受益于同一套能力体系（“取胜之道”）。与其追逐市场机会，不如让机会匹配你独有的能力系统。
def_en: Strategy should rest on a few reinforcing, world-class differentiating capabilities that all the businesses draw on (a coherent 'way to play'). Rather than chasing market opportunities, fit opportunities to the distinctive capability system you already own.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: capability 源自拉丁 capere（抓取、容纳）——能力即“能抓住做成之事”；能力驱动即让战略从“我们擅长什么”出发。

synonyms.sentence: The way to play is how you create value; the capabilities make it real.
The coherence premium rewards firms whose businesses share one capability system.

example: IKEA's low-cost design, flat-pack logistics and store experience are one reinforcing capability system — each business decision draws on the same source of advantage.

def_zh: 格鲁夫:某种10倍级的力量（技术、监管、客户行为）根本改变行业基础规则的时刻——旧战略突然失效，企业要么及时转向，要么衰落。“唯有偏执狂才能生存。”
def_en: Andy Grove's term for the moment a 10x force (technology, regulation, customer behaviour) fundamentally changes an industry's rules — the old strategy suddenly fails, and a firm must pivot in time or decline. 'Only the paranoid survive.'

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: inflection point 借自微积分（曲线凹凸转变之点）——格鲁夫把数学术语搬入管理，指趋势方向突变的时刻。

synonyms.sentence: A 10x force is change so large that adapting slowly is the same as not adapting.
The pivot is what a strategic inflection point demands and inertia resists.

example: The shift to smartphones was a strategic inflection point: firms that treated it as a sustaining trend, not a rule-change, are footnotes now.

def_zh: 里斯:在极度不确定下，创业是一连串“构建-衡量-学习”循环。用最小可行产品（MVP）低成本验证假设，据真实数据决定坚持还是转向（pivot），把浪费降到最低。
def_en: Eric Ries: under extreme uncertainty, a startup is a series of build-measure-learn loops. Test hypotheses cheaply with a minimum viable product (MVP), then use real data to decide whether to persevere or pivot — minimising waste.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: lean 借自丰田“精益生产”（消除浪费）——里斯把制造业的减废哲学搬到创业:别造没人要的东西。

synonyms.sentence: Build-measure-learn is the scientific method with a burn rate.
Validated learning is progress you can prove, not features you can demo.

example: Rather than spend a year building, a lean startup ships a rough MVP in weeks to learn whether anyone wants it — before the money runs out.

def_zh: 能以最小努力完成一轮“构建-衡量-学习”的最简版本产品——目的不是最小的产品，而是最快的学习。用它验证核心假设，避免在无人验证的功能上浪费资源。
def_en: The simplest version of a product that completes one build-measure-learn loop with least effort — the goal is not the smallest product but the fastest learning. Use it to test the core hypothesis before wasting resources on unvalidated features.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: viable 源自法语 vie（生命）——“可行”即“活得下去”；MVP 即“刚好能活着验证的最小产品”。

synonyms.sentence: A proof of concept shows it can work; an MVP shows someone will use it.
A smoke test sells the product before it exists, to see if anyone buys.

example: An MVP can be a landing page with a buy button: if nobody clicks, you've saved a year of building the wrong thing.

def_zh: 产品恰好满足了一个强需求市场，以致增长开始自我拉动——用户留存、口碑、拉动式需求。达到 PMF 前，唯一要务是寻找它；达到后，重心转向增长。创业的分水岭。
def_en: The point where a product so well satisfies a strong-demand market that growth starts pulling itself — retention holds, word of mouth spreads, demand pulls. Before PMF the only job is to find it; after PMF the job becomes growth. The watershed of a startup.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 由投资人 Marc Andreessen 普及——“市场拉着产品从你手里跑”的那一刻，是他给创业者的唯一北极星。

synonyms.sentence: You feel the pull of product-market fit; you don't have to argue for it.
A retention curve that flattens above zero is PMF's clearest fingerprint.

example: Founders chasing growth before product-market fit pour fuel on a fire that isn't lit — the retention curve tells the truth the pitch deck won't.

def_zh: 市场规模的三个同心层:TAM 总可服务市场（若人人都买）、SAM 可服务市场（你的产品与渠道实际能触达）、SOM 可获得市场（近期现实能拿下的份额）。投资人用它检验野心与现实。
def_en: Three nested measures of market size: TAM (total addressable market, if everyone bought), SAM (serviceable available market, what your product and channels can actually reach), SOM (serviceable obtainable market, the share you can realistically win near-term). Investors use it to test ambition against reality.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: 三个首字母缩略（Total/Serviceable Available/Serviceable Obtainable Market）——创业融资演示的标准“市场三段论”。

synonyms.sentence: Market sizing is where optimism and arithmetic have their first fight.
Bottom-up sizing (units × price × buyers) beats a top-down 'if we get 1% of China'.

example: A pitch claiming a trillion-dollar TAM but no credible path to a modest SOM signals ambition untethered from a real go-to-market.

def_zh: 不在既有品类里争第一，而是定义一个新品类并成为其代名词（如 CRM、云计算、电动车高端市场）。品类王往往攫取该品类绝大部分经济价值——先教育市场，再统治市场。
def_en: Rather than compete for first place in an existing category, define a new one and become synonymous with it (CRM, cloud, premium EVs). The category king typically captures most of the category's economic value — educate the market, then rule it.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: category 源自希腊 katēgoria（陈述、指控）——品类即“把事物归为一类的陈述”；创造品类即发明一种新的归类方式。

synonyms.sentence: The category king writes the definition everyone else must answer to.
Category creation sells a point of view before it sells a product.

example: Instead of selling 'better sales software', a firm names a new category and becomes the default answer when buyers ask for it.

def_zh: 战略时机之辩:先发者承担市场教育成本、锁定资源与标准；快速跟随者以更低成本学习先发者的错误、在需求成熟时精准出击。答案取决于网络效应强度、模仿难度与执行力。
def_en: The timing debate: first movers bear the cost of educating the market and lock up resources and standards; fast followers learn from the pioneer's mistakes cheaply and strike when demand matures. The answer depends on network-effect strength, imitability and execution.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

pron: first mover versus fast follower /fərst ˈmuvər ˈvərsəz fæst ˈfɑloʊər/
etymology: fast follower 是1990年代对“先发优势”神话的修正——很多市场里，第二个到、第一个做对的人赢。

synonyms.sentence: The pioneer's penalty is paying to teach a market your successor will inherit.
Timing advantage is knowing which markets reward patience and which punish it.

example: In hardware, fast followers often win: they skip the pioneer's costly dead ends and ship the version the market was actually waiting for.

def_zh: 霍夫曼:在赢家通吃市场中，以牺牲效率换取速度、抢先达到主导规模。明知会犯错、会烧钱，仍优先扩张而非完善——因为在这类市场，慢即是死。风险极高的特定策略。
def_en: Reid Hoffman's term: in winner-take-all markets, prioritise speed over efficiency to reach dominant scale first. Accept mistakes and cash burn, choosing growth over perfection — because in such markets, slow is fatal. A high-risk strategy for specific conditions.

sources.chapter: Growth & Frameworks 增长与框架

etymology: blitz（德语“闪电”，源自 Blitzkrieg 闪电战）+ scaling——以军事闪电战之名喻不计代价的极速扩张。

synonyms.sentence: Grow-at-all-costs works only where the prize is a monopoly, not a margin.
A land grab treats market share as territory to be seized before it is defended.

example: Blitzscaling makes sense for a network-effects market where the winner takes all — and is reckless everywhere else, where it just burns capital.
nuance: 闪电扩张只适用于赢家通吃、网络效应强的市场；在正常市场中它只是“烧钱换伪增长”。多数创业失败源于误用此策略。

def_zh: 麦肯锡分析基石:把一个问题拆成若干子项，彼此不重叠（互斥）、合起来无遗漏（穷尽）。MECE 的拆分让分析既不重复劳动，也不留死角，是结构化思考的第一纪律。
def_en: McKinsey's analytic bedrock: break a problem into parts that don't overlap (mutually exclusive) and together miss nothing (collectively exhaustive). A MECE cut ensures analysis neither double-counts nor leaves gaps — the first discipline of structured thinking.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 由 Barbara Minto 在麦肯锡提出的缩略语（读作 'mee-see'）——结构化沟通与分析的核心检验。

synonyms.sentence: A clean decomposition is one where every item has exactly one home.
'No gaps, no overlaps' is MECE translated into plain English.

example: Splitting customers by 'young, old, and loyal' fails MECE — the buckets overlap; 'under 30 / 30-plus' passes.

def_zh: 把核心问题逐层拆成 MECE 的子问题，形成一棵树——每层都是对上层的完整分解，直到叶子成为可分析、可验证的具体假设。它把模糊的大问题变成有序的分析路线图。
def_en: Breaking a core question into MECE sub-questions, level by level, into a tree — each layer a complete decomposition of the one above, until the leaves are specific, testable hypotheses. It turns a fuzzy big question into an ordered analytic roadmap.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: tree（树）借自图论——问题被画成根-枝-叶的层级结构；亦称 logic tree 或 driver tree。

synonyms.sentence: A logic tree shows the whole problem on one page and where each branch leads.
A driver tree decomposes a metric into the levers that actually move it.

example: 'Why are profits down?' becomes an issue tree: revenue (price × volume) versus cost (fixed versus variable) — each branch a place to dig.

def_zh: 麦肯锡工作方式:一开始就提出对答案的最佳猜测（初始假设），再设计分析去验证或推翻它，而非漫无目的地收集数据。它让稀缺的时间聚焦于真正影响结论的少数关键分析。
def_en: The McKinsey way of working: state a best guess at the answer up front (the initial hypothesis), then design analysis to confirm or kill it — rather than gathering data aimlessly. It focuses scarce time on the few analyses that actually move the conclusion.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: hypothesis 源自希腊 hypo（在下）+ thesis（放置）——“假设”即“放在论证之下的前提”；科学方法进咨询室。

synonyms.sentence: The day-one answer is a hypothesis you fully expect the data to revise.
Answer-first work designs the analysis backward from the claim it must test.

example: Instead of boiling the ocean, a team states a hypothesis — 'the loss is in one region' — then runs only the analysis that would refute it.
nuance: 关键在“可证伪”:好假设明确指出“什么证据会推翻它”。假设驱动不是先入为主，而是先立靶再射箭、随证据修正。

def_zh: 明托的沟通法则:先给结论（塔尖），再用若干 MECE 的论据支撑，每个论据下再有证据——自上而下。读者先知道答案，再看理由。咨询汇报与商务写作的黄金结构。
def_en: Minto's communication rule: lead with the conclusion (the apex), support it with a few MECE arguments, each backed by evidence — top-down. The reader gets the answer first, then the reasons. The gold standard for consulting decks and business writing.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: Barbara Minto 1970年代在麦肯锡创立——把论点结构画成金字塔:一个主张，几根支柱，一层证据。

synonyms.sentence: An answer-first structure respects the reader's time and the executive's patience.
Top-down communication states the 'so what' before the 'how we know'.

example: A partner opens with the recommendation, then three supporting reasons — the pyramid principle means the busy client gets the point in the first sentence.

def_zh: 帕累托观察:约80%的结果来自约20%的原因——80%利润来自20%客户，80%缺陷来自20%环节。咨询用它聚焦少数高杠杆点，避免在低影响细节上均匀用力。
def_en: Pareto's observation: roughly 80% of outcomes come from 20% of causes — 80% of profit from 20% of customers, 80% of defects from 20% of steps. Consultants use it to focus on the few high-leverage points and avoid spreading effort evenly over low-impact detail.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 以意大利经济学家 Vilfredo Pareto 命名（他发现意大利80%土地属20%人口）——后被 Juran 推广为通用管理法则。

synonyms.sentence: The vital few outweigh the trivial many, if you can tell them apart.
The Pareto principle is permission to ignore most of the list.

example: An 80/20 cut of the product line often reveals that a fifth of SKUs earn nearly all the profit — and the rest just add complexity.

def_zh: 组织有效性的七要素:战略、结构、系统（硬三要素）+ 共同价值观、技能、人员、风格（软四要素）。核心洞见:变革要成功，七者须相互对齐——只改结构而不改文化与技能，注定失败。
def_en: Seven elements of organisational effectiveness: strategy, structure, systems (the hard three) plus shared values, skills, staff, style (the soft four). The insight: change succeeds only when all seven align — reorganising the boxes without shifting culture and skills is doomed.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 1980年 Peters、Waterman 与 Pascale 在麦肯锡创立——七个以 S 开头的要素，故名 7S。

synonyms.sentence: Organisational alignment is when the strategy and the culture are telling the same story.
Hard factors are easy to change and soft ones are where transformations die.

example: A new strategy failed because the 7S were misaligned — the structure and systems still rewarded the old behaviour the strategy needed to end.

def_zh: 对每一项分析、每一页幻灯片反复追问:“所以呢？对决策意味着什么？”——把数据提炼为洞见、把洞见提炼为建议。没通过 so-what 测试的内容，只是信息，不是价值。
def_en: Relentlessly asking of every analysis and every slide: 'so what? what does it mean for the decision?' — distilling data into insight and insight into recommendation. Anything that fails the so-what test is information, not value.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 咨询行话——最简短也最锋利的自我审查:逼作者从“我发现了什么”走到“这对客户意味着什么”。

synonyms.sentence: An insight is a fact that has survived the 'so what?' question.
The implication is what the client should do differently because of the number.

example: 'Sales fell 8%' is a fact; the so-what — 'so we're losing the price war and must reposition' — is the insight the client is paying for.

def_zh: 每页幻灯片的标题应陈述该页的核心结论（“成本集中在物流环节”），而非描述内容（“成本分解”）。把所有行动标题连读，就是完整的故事线——读者只看标题即懂全局。
def_en: Each slide's title should state the page's conclusion ('cost is concentrated in logistics'), not describe its contents ('cost breakdown'). Read the action titles in sequence and they form the whole storyline — a reader who skims only the headlines still gets the argument.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 亦称 message titles 或 takeaway titles——“行动”指标题本身传递一个可据以行动的结论。

synonyms.sentence: A message title argues; a label title merely announces.
If the takeaway headline needs the chart to be understood, rewrite it.

collocations: read the titles as a story
example: Change 'Regional Sales Data' to 'The North drives 70% of decline' — the action title makes the slide argue instead of describe.

def_zh: 在做分析之前先搭出汇报的逻辑主线（骨架幻灯片/ghost deck）——用一串前后咬合的结论把听众从问题引向建议。先定故事，再用分析填充与检验，而非攒完数据再拼故事。
def_en: Laying out the report's logical spine before doing the analysis (a ghost deck of skeleton slides) — a chain of interlocking conclusions that carries the audience from problem to recommendation. Fix the story first, then fill and test it with analysis, rather than assembling a story after the data piles up.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: story（故事）+ line（线）——把论证想象成一条叙事线；ghost deck 指内容尚空、只有标题的“幽灵”演示稿。

synonyms.sentence: A ghost deck tests the argument before a single chart is built.
A storyboard is the film-maker's word for what consultants call the storyline.

example: Before crunching numbers, the team storylines the whole deck as headlines — spotting the gap in the argument while it's still cheap to fix.

def_zh: 项目伊始就基于已有知识给出对结论的最佳初步假设，并据此设计整个工作计划。它随证据不断修正，但从第一天就提供方向，避免团队在无靶状态下漫游。
def_en: On day one, state the best preliminary hypothesis for the conclusion from existing knowledge, and design the whole workplan around it. It gets revised as evidence arrives, but it provides direction from the start — sparing the team an aimless wander.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 咨询行话——与“先收集所有数据再想结论”相反，强调 day one 就有一个可被推翻的方向。

synonyms.sentence: An initial hypothesis is a compass, not a conclusion.
The leading hypothesis earns its place until the data unseats it.

example: A partner's day-one answer — 'this is a pricing problem, not a cost problem' — shapes the workplan, then bends as the analysis talks back.

def_zh: 分析是把问题拆开，综合是把发现重新拼成一个连贯的答案。咨询的价值多在综合——从一堆分析中提炼出“所以，你该这么做”的整体判断。分析人人会，综合见功力。
def_en: Analysis breaks a problem apart; synthesis reassembles the findings into one coherent answer. Much of consulting's value is in synthesis — distilling a pile of analyses into a single 'so here's what you should do' judgement. Anyone can analyse; synthesis is where mastery shows.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: synthesis 源自希腊 syn（一起）+ thesis（放置）——综合即“把碎片放回一起”；与 analysis（希腊“拆开”）成对。

synonyms.sentence: An integrated answer is more than the sum of its slides.
Sense-making turns a data room into a decision.

example: Ten analyses each say something; synthesis is the partner's ability to say the one thing all ten together mean for the CEO.

def_zh: 把一个关键指标（如利润、ROIC）逐层分解为可量化的驱动因素:利润=收入−成本，收入=单价×销量……直到叶子是可管理的操作杠杆。它把抽象目标翻译成具体可拨动的旋钮。
def_en: Decomposing a key metric (profit, ROIC) into quantified drivers, level by level: profit = revenue − cost, revenue = price × volume, and so on, until the leaves are manageable operational levers. It translates an abstract target into concrete knobs you can turn.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: driver（驱动因素）+ tree——与 issue tree 同形，但节点是可量化的数值关系而非问题。

synonyms.sentence: A value driver tree shows exactly which lever, moved how far, hits the target.
A KPI tree connects the boardroom metric to the shop-floor action.

example: A driver tree on ROIC shows margin and capital turnover as the two branches — telling management whether to chase pricing or asset efficiency.

def_zh: 把自身绩效与最佳实践者（同业、跨业、内部单元）系统对比，找出差距与改进空间。对标提供“可能达到的水平”的外部参照，把内部争论从意见变为事实。
def_en: Systematically comparing your performance against best practitioners (peers, other industries, internal units) to expose gaps and improvement potential. Benchmarking supplies an external reference for what's achievable, turning internal debate from opinion into fact.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: benchmark 源自测量员在工作台（bench）上刻的基准标记（mark）——引申为衡量绩效的参照点。

synonyms.sentence: A best-in-class comparison turns 'we're doing fine' into 'fine compared with whom?'
The performance gap is the distance between you and what's demonstrably possible.

example: Benchmarking a plant against the industry's top quartile reveals a 20% cost gap that internal reviews, comparing only to last year, never saw.

def_zh: 把优势、劣势（内部）与机会、威胁（外部）列成四象限的经典盘点工具。简单易用但易流于罗列——真正的价值在于由此推出战略行动（用优势抓机会、补劣势御威胁），而非清单本身。
def_en: The classic four-quadrant stocktake of Strengths, Weaknesses (internal) and Opportunities, Threats (external). Simple and popular but easily degenerates into listing — its real value is deriving strategic actions (use strengths on opportunities, fix weaknesses against threats), not the list itself.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: Strengths/Weaknesses/Opportunities/Threats 首字母缩略——1960年代 Albert Humphrey 在斯坦福研究项目中提出。

synonyms.sentence: An internal-external scan pairs what you have with what the world offers.
Situational analysis is SWOT with the discipline to draw conclusions.

example: A SWOT is worthless as a list and valuable as a bridge: match a strength to an opportunity and it becomes an actionable strategy.
nuance: SWOT 常被批为“想到什么写什么”的软工具——纪律在于每一项都要 so-what，且区分“内部可控”（S/W）与“外部给定”（O/T）。

def_zh: 扫描宏观环境的六类外部力量:政治、经济、社会、技术、环境、法律。用于识别影响行业的大趋势与结构性变化，是情景规划与市场进入分析的输入。
def_en: A scan of six macro-environmental forces: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, Legal. Used to spot the big trends and structural shifts shaping an industry — an input to scenario planning and market-entry analysis.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: Political/Economic/Social/Technological/Environmental/Legal 首字母缩略——早期为 PEST，后加入环境与法律两维。

synonyms.sentence: A macro-environment scan looks past competitors to the tides moving all of them.
The big forces of PESTEL reshape industries faster than any single rival.

example: Before entering a foreign market, a PESTEL analysis flags the regulatory (L) and political (P) risks that a pure competitive analysis would miss.

def_zh: 卡普兰与诺顿:绩效不能只看财务，应从财务、客户、内部流程、学习与成长四个视角平衡衡量——把长期能力建设与短期财务结果同时纳入管理仪表盘。战略执行的经典工具。
def_en: Kaplan and Norton: performance shouldn't be measured by finance alone but balanced across four perspectives — financial, customer, internal process, learning and growth — putting long-term capability-building and short-term results on the same dashboard. A classic tool for executing strategy.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: balanced（平衡）指在财务与非财务、短期与长期、内部与外部之间求平衡——纠正“只看季度利润”的近视。

synonyms.sentence: A strategy map draws the cause-and-effect from learning to profit.
Leading indicators warn; lagging indicators confirm — a scorecard needs both.

example: A balanced scorecard stops managers from hitting this quarter's profit by gutting the training and R&D that drive next decade's.

def_zh: 源自英特尔、由谷歌发扬的目标管理法:设定少数鼓舞人心的定性目标（Objective），每个配3–5个可量化的关键结果（Key Results）衡量进展。强调聚焦、对齐、透明与延展性目标。
def_en: A goal-setting method from Intel, popularised by Google: set a few inspiring qualitative Objectives, each with 3–5 measurable Key Results tracking progress. It stresses focus, alignment, transparency and stretch targets.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: Objectives and Key Results 缩略——Andy Grove 在英特尔创立，John Doerr 带入谷歌后风行科技界。

synonyms.sentence: Stretch goals aim for 0.7, because hitting 1.0 means you aimed too low.
Aligned objectives let a thousand people pull in one direction without a memo each.

collocations: the objective and its key results
example: An OKR pairs an ambitious objective — 'delight new users' — with hard key results like activation rate, so inspiration stays measurable.

def_zh: 衡量组织、团队或流程是否达成目标的量化指标。好的 KPI 少而关键、可操作、难以被操纵。危险在于“度量什么就得到什么”——错误的 KPI 会诱导错误行为。
def_en: Quantified measures of whether an organisation, team or process is hitting its goals. Good KPIs are few, actionable and hard to game. The danger: you get what you measure — the wrong KPI drives the wrong behaviour.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: performance 源自古法语 parfournir（完成、执行）——KPI 即“衡量执行成效的关键指标”。

synonyms.sentence: Metrics that matter change behaviour; vanity metrics only decorate reports.
Goodhart's law warns: when a measure becomes a target, it stops measuring well.

example: A call centre rewarded on 'calls per hour' hit the KPI by rushing customers off — a textbook case of Goodhart's law punishing a lazy metric.
nuance: 古德哈特定律:一旦指标变成考核目标，它就失去了作为指标的意义（人会优化指标而非目标本身）。设 KPI 须防被“钻空子”。

def_zh: 一个能最好地捕捉产品为客户创造的核心价值、并与长期营收挂钩的单一指标（如“每周活跃协作用户数”）。全公司围绕它对齐，避免被众多次要指标牵扯分散。
def_en: The single metric that best captures the core value a product delivers to customers and correlates with long-term revenue (say, 'weekly active collaborating users'). The whole company aligns around it, resisting the pull of many secondary metrics.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: north star（北极星）——航海中指引方向的恒定之星，喻团队应始终对准的那一个指标。

synonyms.sentence: The one metric that matters this quarter should embarrass every distraction.
A north star is a value proxy: move it and customers are genuinely better off.

example: A messaging app picks 'messages sent per active user' as its north star — grow that honestly and revenue follows, because it proxies real value.

def_zh: 厘清决策与任务中谁扮演什么角色:负责执行（Responsible）、最终担责（Accountable）、需咨询（Consulted）、需知会（Informed）。每项任务只应有一个 A，避免“人人有责等于无人负责”。
def_en: Clarifies who plays what role in a decision or task: Responsible (does the work), Accountable (owns the outcome), Consulted (gives input), Informed (kept in the loop). Each task should have exactly one A — lest 'everyone's responsible' means no one is.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: Responsible/Accountable/Consulted/Informed 首字母缩略——项目管理与组织设计厘清决策权的标准工具。

synonyms.sentence: Decision rights, made explicit, end the meetings that exist only to relitigate them.
'A single throat to choke' is the blunt version of one accountable owner.

example: A stalled project unblocked the moment a RACI revealed three people thought they were Accountable and none actually owned the call.

def_zh: 咨询的信念:用数据与证据而非资历、直觉或办公室政治来定胜负。“In God we trust; all others must bring data.” 事实基础让最年轻的分析师也能挑战最资深的高管——只要证据在手。
def_en: A consulting creed: settle questions with data and evidence, not seniority, intuition or office politics. 'In God we trust; all others must bring data.' A fact base lets the most junior analyst challenge the most senior executive — provided the evidence is in hand.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: fact 源自拉丁 factum（已做成之事）——事实即“确已发生之事”；fact-based 即让结论落地于可查证之事。

synonyms.sentence: An evidence-led culture lets the data outrank the hierarchy.
A shared fact base turns a debate of opinions into a search for the answer.

example: A fact-based culture means a 24-year-old can say 'the numbers disagree' to a division head — and be thanked, not fired.

def_zh: 精确核算服务每个客户、渠道或订单的全部成本（含物流、退货、支持、折扣），而非只看毛利。常揭示“大客户实则亏损”——收入高但服务成本更高。是客户组合与定价优化的基础。
def_en: Precisely accounting for the full cost of serving each customer, channel or order (logistics, returns, support, discounts), not just gross margin. It often reveals that a big customer is actually a loss-maker — high revenue but higher cost-to-serve. The basis for optimising the customer mix and pricing.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: serve 源自拉丁 servire（服务、侍奉）——cost-to-serve 即“为服务某客户所真正花掉的钱”。

synonyms.sentence: Customer profitability, done honestly, reshuffles who your best customers really are.
Activity-based costing traces overhead to the activities that actually cause it.

example: Cost-to-serve analysis showed the biggest account was the least profitable — its discounts and returns consumed every cent of its margin.

def_zh: 从关键问题倒推工作，而非从数据正推结论:先明确“要回答什么问题、什么答案会改变决策”，再只做能推动这些问题的分析。避免“分析瘫痪”与无关数据的堆积。
def_en: Working backward from the key questions rather than forward from data: first pin down what question must be answered and what answer would change the decision, then run only the analysis that moves those questions. It avoids analysis paralysis and heaps of irrelevant data.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: issue 源自古法语 issue（出口、结果）——“议题”即“待解决而通向结论的关口”。

synonyms.sentence: Question-led work refuses to analyse anything that couldn't change the answer.
Analysis paralysis is what happens when data collection has no governing question.

example: Issue-based working kills a week of pointless modelling with one question: 'even if this analysis came out either way, would the client act differently?'

def_zh: 咨询的独特价值主张:训练有素的结构化思考者，即便非行业专家，也能靠方法（拆解、假设、类比、快速学习）在陌生领域快速产出洞见。通才用方法弥补知识，专家用知识深挖——两者互补。
def_en: Consulting's distinctive proposition: a trained structured thinker, even without industry expertise, can produce insight fast in an unfamiliar field through method — decomposition, hypotheses, analogy, rapid learning. The generalist substitutes method for knowledge; the expert digs deeper with it. The two are complementary.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: generalist 源自拉丁 generalis（属于全类的，genus 类）——通才即“跨类通用”者，与 specialist（专于一类）相对。

synonyms.sentence: Structured problem-solving is the generalist's replacement for domain memory.
The T-shaped professional pairs broad method with one deep spike.

example: A generalist team cracked a chemicals problem the insiders couldn't — not by knowing more chemistry, but by asking the structured questions insiders had stopped asking.
nuance: 通才的风险是“懂皮毛的自负”——好的通才始终配合行业专家，用方法组织专家的知识，而非替代它。

def_zh: 把问题拆解回最基本、不可再拆的真理，再从头推演，而非依赖类比或“行业惯例”。绕开“大家都这么做”的路径依赖，常能发现被沿袭假设掩盖的机会（如马斯克重算火箭成本）。
def_en: Breaking a problem down to its most basic, irreducible truths and reasoning up from there, instead of relying on analogy or 'how the industry does it'. Bypassing the path dependence of received wisdom often surfaces opportunities that inherited assumptions concealed (Musk recomputing rocket costs).

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 源自亚里士多德 archē（本原、第一因）——第一性原理即“无法再由他物推出的最初前提”。

synonyms.sentence: Reasoning from scratch asks what the physics allows, not what the market assumes.
Question the assumption everyone stopped noticing and the opportunity appears.

example: First-principles thinking recomputed battery cost from raw material prices — revealing that 'batteries are expensive' was an assumption, not a law.

def_zh: 把竞争当作参与者相互预判、相互反应的博弈来分析:纳什均衡、囚徒困境、可信承诺、信号与威慑。核心提醒:你的最优选择取决于对手会如何回应——不能只做单方最优化。
def_en: Analysing competition as a game where players anticipate and react to each other: Nash equilibrium, the prisoner's dilemma, credible commitment, signalling and deterrence. The core reminder: your best move depends on how rivals will respond — you cannot optimise one-sidedly.

sources.chapter: The Consulting Method 咨询方法

etymology: 由 von Neumann 与 Morgenstern（1944）奠基——把战略互动数学化；Nash 均衡使其成为经济学与商业的通用语。

synonyms.sentence: Strategic interaction means your payoff has someone else's decision inside it.
A credible commitment changes rivals' behaviour precisely because you can't back out.

example: Two rivals stuck in a price-war prisoner's dilemma both lose — game theory shows why a credible commitment to hold price can rescue both.
nuance: 商业博弈多为“非零和”:合作可扩大蛋糕（正和），并非只有你死我活。误把一切当零和是新手常见错误。

def_zh: 金融第一原理:今天的一元比未来的一元更值钱——因可投资生息、有通胀与风险。一切估值都是把未来现金流按贴现率折回今天。掌握它，才理解利率、投资与估值的全部逻辑。
def_en: Finance's first principle: a dollar today is worth more than a dollar tomorrow — it can be invested to earn a return, and inflation and risk erode the future. All valuation is discounting future cash flows back to today. Grasp it and interest, investing and valuation all follow.

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值

pron: time value of money /taɪm ˈvælju əv ˈməni/
etymology: 源自复利数学；present value（现值）与 future value（终值）是其两端，贴现率是连接二者的桥。

synonyms.sentence: Present value is tomorrow's cash translated into today's language.
Discounting is compounding run in reverse.

example: A promised million in ten years is worth far less today — the time value of money is why 'I'll pay you later' always costs something.

def_zh: 把一个项目未来所有现金流按资本成本贴现到今天，再减去初始投资。NPV>0 意味着项目创造价值、应投资。它是资本预算的黄金法则:接受所有正 NPV 项目。
def_en: Discounting all of a project's future cash flows to today at the cost of capital, then subtracting the initial outlay. A positive NPV means the project creates value and should be undertaken. It is capital budgeting's golden rule: accept every positive-NPV project.

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值

etymology: net（净）指减去初始投资后的净额；present value 见前——NPV 即“扣除成本后的现值”。

synonyms.sentence: The NPV rule is the cleanest value-creation test finance offers.
A positive NPV means the project earns more than the capital it consumes.

collocations: discount at the cost of capital
example: If a factory's discounted cash flows exceed its build cost, the NPV is positive — finance says build it, regardless of gut feel.
nuance: NPV 优于 IRR:当项目规模或现金流形态不同时，IRR 可能误导，NPV 始终以“创造多少绝对价值”为准。

def_zh: 使项目净现值恰好为零的贴现率——即项目自身的年化回报率。IRR 高于资本成本则值得投。直观易懂，但在非常规现金流或比较不同规模项目时会误导。
def_en: The discount rate at which a project's NPV equals zero — the project's own annualised return. If the IRR exceeds the cost of capital, the project is worth doing. Intuitive, but it misleads with unconventional cash flows or when comparing projects of different sizes.

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值

pron: internal rate of return /ˌɪnˈtərnəl reɪt əv rɪˈtərn/
etymology: internal 指“项目内部产生”的回报率，不依赖外部假设的贴现率——它是解方程解出来的那个率。

synonyms.sentence: A project clears the hurdle rate or it doesn't get funded.
IRR is a money-weighted return: it cares when the cash arrives, not just how much.

example: Private equity funds live and die by IRR — a 25% IRR means every dollar invested compounded at 25% a year until it came back.
nuance: IRR 假设中间现金流按 IRR 再投资（常不现实），且忽略绝对规模——20%回报的小项目 IRR 高，却可能不如10%回报的大项目创造的价值多。

def_zh: 内在价值估值的核心方法:预测企业未来自由现金流，用加权平均资本成本贴现到今天，加上终值的现值，得出企业价值。理论最严谨，但对假设（增长率、贴现率、终值）极度敏感。
def_en: The core intrinsic-value method: forecast a firm's future free cash flows, discount them to today at the weighted average cost of capital, add the present value of the terminal value, and you have enterprise value. The most rigorous approach — and acutely sensitive to its assumptions (growth, discount rate, terminal value).

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值
权益与固定收益 Equity & Fixed Income

etymology: discount 源自拉丁 dis-（去除）+ computare（计算）——折现即“算掉时间与风险的价值损耗”。

synonyms.sentence: Intrinsic valuation prices a business by what it will produce, not what others will pay.
A DCF is only as honest as the cash flow forecast beneath it.

example: A DCF can justify almost any price by nudging the growth and discount rates — which is why analysts triangulate it with market multiples.
nuance: DCF 的致命弱点:终值常占估值的60–80%，而终值又建立在最不确定的远期假设上——“精确的错误”。须做敏感性分析。

def_zh: 企业在维持与扩张经营所需投资之后，真正可自由分配给资本提供者（股东、债权人）的现金:经营现金流减资本开支。“利润是观点，现金是事实”——FCF 是估值与偿债能力的硬通货。
def_en: The cash a firm truly has left for its capital providers (shareholders, lenders) after the investment needed to maintain and grow the business: operating cash flow minus capex. 'Profit is an opinion, cash is a fact' — FCF is the hard currency of valuation and solvency.

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值
权益与固定收益 Equity & Fixed Income

etymology: free 指“自由支配”——扣除必要投资后剩下的、可自由分给资本方的现金，故名。

synonyms.sentence: Cash generation is the reality check accrual profit sometimes fails.
Buffett's 'owner earnings' is free cash flow seen through a shareholder's eyes.

example: A firm can report rising profits while free cash flow bleeds — inventory and receivables swallowing the earnings before they become cash.
nuance: FCFF（对全体资本方）与 FCFE（仅对股东）须区分:前者贴现用 WACC 得企业价值，后者贴现用股权成本得股权价值。混用会算错。

def_zh: 资本提供者要求的最低回报率——债权人要利息，股东要与风险相称的回报。它既是投资项目的“门槛率”，也是贴现率。低于资本成本的回报即在摧毁价值。
def_en: The minimum return capital providers demand — lenders want interest, shareholders want a return matching the risk. It is both the hurdle rate for investments and the discount rate for valuation. Earning below the cost of capital destroys value.

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值

etymology: capital 源自拉丁 caput（头→本金）——资本成本即“使用这笔本金的代价”。

synonyms.sentence: The hurdle rate is the cost of capital wearing its decision-making hat.
The required return is what investors could earn elsewhere at equal risk.

example: A business earning 6% on capital that costs 9% is quietly destroying value every year, no matter how profitable it looks.

def_zh: 企业债务成本与股权成本按各自市值权重加权的综合资本成本，是 DCF 中贴现企业自由现金流的标准贴现率。因债务利息可抵税，适度举债能降低 WACC——但过高杠杆又抬升风险成本。
def_en: The blended cost of a firm's debt and equity, weighted by their market values — the standard discount rate for a firm's free cash flows in a DCF. Because interest is tax-deductible, moderate debt lowers WACC — but excessive leverage raises risk costs again.

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值
权益与固定收益 Equity & Fixed Income

etymology: Weighted Average Cost of Capital 缩略（读作 'wack'）——把不同来源的资金成本按比例调和为一个数。

synonyms.sentence: The blended cost of capital mixes cheap debt with expensive equity.
In most DCFs, the discount rate simply is the WACC.

example: Because interest is tax-deductible, adding some debt lowers WACC — up to the point where lenders start pricing in default risk.

def_zh: 股东要求的回报率——因股东承担最末位、最高的风险，其要求高于债权人。通常用资本资产定价模型（CAPM）估算:无风险利率 + β × 股权风险溢价。
def_en: The return shareholders demand — higher than lenders' because equity holders bear the last, riskiest claim. Usually estimated with the CAPM: the risk-free rate plus beta times the equity risk premium.

sources.chapter: Valuation 估值
权益与固定收益 Equity & Fixed Income

etymology: equity 源自拉丁 aequitas（公平）——股权本指“公平分享剩余”的权利；其成本即股东要的补偿。

synonyms.sentence: The required equity return is what shareholders could earn on equally risky bets elsewhere.
The CAPM rate turns a stock's market risk into a required return.

example: A volatile tech stock's cost of equity might be 12% while a utility's is 7% — beta captures how much market risk each shareholder bears.

def_zh: 金融理论基石:一项资产的预期回报 = 无风险利率 + β ×（市场回报−无风险利率）。核心思想是只有无法分散的系统性风险（β）才应获得补偿，可分散的个体风险不该被定价。
